index_title: Data Transmission: From Wires to Clouds
index_subtitle: Modern data transmission methods: wired, wireless, and future interfaces
intro_text1: In the modern world, information is transmitted using various physical media and technologies. The choice depends on many factors: distance, speed, noise immunity, budget, and ease of use.
intro_text2: On this site you will find detailed descriptions of key technologies: wired lines (copper and fiber), wireless solutions, and popular device interfaces.
copper_desc: Electrical signals over metallic conductors. An inexpensive and simple way for local networks up to 100 meters.
fiber_desc: Light pulses through glass fibers. Gigabit and terabit speeds over hundreds of kilometers.
wifi_desc: Local wireless networks. 802.11ax (Wi-Fi 6) provides speeds up to 9.6 Gbit/s.
bt_desc: Short-range communication (up to 100 m) for peripherals.
cellular_desc: Mobile networks, satellite internet (Starlink). Global coverage.
usb_desc: Universal Serial Bus. Versions: USB 2.0 (480 Mbit/s), USB 3.2 (20 Gbit/s), USB4 (40 Gbit/s). Connector types: A, B, C.
video_desc: Digital interfaces for high-resolution video and audio transmission.
thunderbolt_desc: High-speed interface (up to 40 Gbit/s) compatible with USB-C.
footer_text: © 2025 Data Transmission: From Copper to Fiber. Educational Project.
